title: Tire treads having increasing block lengths from shoulder to center of tread
abstract: Particular embodiments provide a tire tread having a plurality of tread elements and a plurality of lateral discontinuities arranged along the tread width and tread length, each of the plurality of tread elements being spaced apart by one of the plurality of lateral discontinuities, where the tread width is divided into a plurality of regions, the plurality of regions including: a pair of outermost regions, each outermost region being arranged closest to one of the pair of opposing lateral sides of the tread, and at least one centermost region is arranged closest to the longitudinal centerline of the tread. At least one of the tread elements arranged in the at least one centermost region has a length greater than a length of at least one of tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A tire tread comprising: a tread length extending in a longitudinal direction, a tread width extending in a lateral direction, a tread thickness extending in a depthwise direction from an outer, ground-engaging side of the tread, each of the longitudinal direction, the lateral direction, and the depthwise direction being perpendicular to one another, the tread width extending between a pair of opposing sides of the tread each arranged at a widthwise extent of the outer, ground-engaging side, and a longitudinal centerline extending in the tread length in the longitudinal direction midway across the tread width; a plurality of tread elements and a plurality of lateral discontinuities arranged along the tread width and tread length, each of the plurality of tread elements being spaced apart by one of the plurality of lateral discontinuities; where the tread width is divided into a plurality of regions, the plurality of regions including: (1) a pair of outermost regions, each outermost region being arranged closest to one of the pair of opposing lateral sides of the tread, and (2) at least one centermost region is arranged closest to the longitudinal centerline of the tread, where at least one of the tread elements arranged in the at least one centermost region has a length greater than a length of at least one of tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions. 2. The tire tread of claim 1 , where the tire tread includes a plurality of ribs extending in the longitudinal direction of the tire tread, where a portion of the plurality of tread elements are arranged side-by-side in the longitudinal direction of the tread in each rib of the plurality of ribs, where the plurality of ribs includes a pair of outermost ribs, each rib of the pair of outermost ribs arranged closest to one of the pair of opposing lateral sides of the tread of the plurality of ribs, and at least one centermost rib arranged closest to the longitudinal centerline of the tread of the plurality of ribs, where one rib of the pair of outermost ribs is arranged in one of the pair of outermost regions and the other rib of the pair of outermost ribs is arranged in the 

 other of the pair of outermost ribs, and where the center rib is arranged in the center region. 3. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 and 2 further comprising: an intermediate region arranged between the centermost region and at least one of the pair of outermost regions, at least one of the tread elements in each intermediate region having a length greater than at least one of the tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions and smaller than at least one of the tread elements arranged in the centermost region. 4. The tire tread of claim 3 further comprising: one or more intermediate ribs arranged between the at least one centermost rib and the pair of outermost ribs, each of the one or more intermediate ribs being arranged in the intermediate rib. 5. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 4, where each of the lateral discontinuities forms a sipe or a groove. 6. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 4, where each of the plurality of lateral discontinuities forms a sipe. 7. The tire tread of claim 6, where each the sipe thickness for each sipe is configured to articulate between an open configuration and a closed configuration as the tire rotates during tire operation. 8. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 7, where each of the lateral discontinuities extend into the tread from the outer, ground-engaging side of the tread. 9. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 8, where the at least one of the tread elements arranged in the at least one centermost region has a length greater than a length of at least one of tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions is arranged within a segment of the tread, the segment of the tread extending the width of the tread and partially in the longitudinal direction of the tread. 10. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 8, where the at least one of the tread elements arranged in the at least one centermost region having a length greater than a length of at least one of tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions is arranged to be within a tire footprint concurrently during tire operation. 11. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 10, where the at least one of the tread elements arranged in the at least one centermost region having a length greater than a length of at least one of tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions is arranged at the same lengthwise location along the tread length. 12. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 11, where an average length of the tread elements arranged in each of the pair of outermost regions is smaller than an average length of the tread elements arranged in the centermost region. 13. The tire tread of claim 12, where the average length of the tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost regions is smaller than an average length of the tread elements arranged in the one or more intermediate regions, and where the average length of the tread elements in the one or more intermediate regions is smaller than the average length of the tread elements arranged in each of the at least one centermost region. 14. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 13, where an average length of the tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost ribs is smaller than an average length of the tread elements arranged in the at least one centermost rib. 15. The tire tread of claim 14, where the average length of the tread elements arranged in the pair of outermost ribs is smaller than an average length of the tread elements arranged in each of the one or more intermediate ribs, and where the average length of the tread elements in the one or more intermediate ribs is smaller than the average length of the tread elements arranged in each of the at least one centermost rib. 16. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 15, where the length for each of the lateral discontinuities extends along a linear or non-linear path. 17. The tire tread of any one of claims 1 to 16, where the depthwise extension for each of the lateral discontinuities extends along a linear or non- linear path.
